danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite variation - ghouls, skeletons, demons rendered with distinct visual designs; larger/more ornate sprites indicate stronger enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies threat level and enemy capability; visual hierarchy communicates danger escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental theme shifts - catacombs with long corridors, caves with non-linear layouts, increasing darkness and Hell aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Deeper levels = greater danger; visual environment communicates proximity to Diablo and narrative threat escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Icon indicator at lower right shows head protection damage status; visual wear on equipped items
danger_signals.meaning: Degraded armor signals vulnerability; player's defensive capability is visually compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite takes visible damage; UI health bar depletes
danger_signals.meaning: Direct visual feedback on character mortality state

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental floor/wall indicators (implied by Rogue trap-disarm mechanic)
danger_signals.meaning: Communicates mechanical danger separate from enemy threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Tristram above-ground area with visible NPCs and structures; distinct from dungeon aesthetic
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for trading, quest-giving, healing; faction-neutral gathering space

safety_signals.visual_form: Loot drops rendered as distinct visual objects; color-coded quality (white-color items mentioned as baseline)
safety_signals.meaning: Power increase and progression; safety through equipment improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: 'Level Up' button UI element indicates character advancement available
safety_signals.meaning: Stat growth and character strengthening; progression signal

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tristram town; NPCs and quest-givers in above-ground areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghouls, skeletons, demons; sprite variation indicates unit type and allegiance to demonic forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Catacombs, caves, Hell dimension; corrupted architecture and darkness increase with depth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brighter, more structured dungeon layouts; catacomb corridors and rooms; fewer/weaker enemy sprites visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies less aggressive; quest opportunities available; NPCs accessible in Tristram

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to cave aesthetics; more non-linear layouts; increased enemy sprite variety and density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stronger enemy encounters; procedurally assigned quests escalate in difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened environments; Hell dimension aesthetics; demonic architecture and corrupted visual themes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory final quests; Diablo encounter; most challenging enemy types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Armor/weapon icons show wear; UI indicators display damage state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player vulnerability increases; repair/replacement becomes necessary for progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Catacombs with long corridors and closed rooms; caves with non-linear layouts; each level type communicates narrative function and enemy type
16 randomly generated dungeon levels descend from structured catacombs toward Hell; visual darkness and architectural corruption increase with depth, communicating descent toward evil source
Tristram town above-ground serves as narrative hub; NPCs and quest-givers visually isolated from dungeon, establishing safe refuge narrative
Different enemy sprites (ghouls, skeletons, demons) appear at specific depth levels; visual design communicates thematic shift from undead to demonic forces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite shows close-quarters stance; high armor visual; heavy weapon/shield equipped; capable of melee damage output
Sprite shows ranged stance; bow equipped; lighter armor visual; positioned for distance attacks
Sprite shows spell-casting stance; staff or wand equipped; minimal armor visual; mana pool indicator visible
Distinct sprite animations for movement and attack; visual differentiation from player character indicates enemy status
Equipment damage icon visible in UI; sprite may show visual wear; reduced defensive capability communicated through UI

notes: Diablo uses primarily sprite-based visual communication. Danger is conveyed through enemy sprite design variation, environmental theme shifts, and UI damage indicators. Factions are distinguished by visual style (demonic vs. human NPCs) and spatial segregation (dungeon vs. town). World state transitions are communicated through procedural level design themes and enemy type distribution. The game relies heavily on visual hierarchy: larger/more ornate sprites indicate stronger threats; deeper dungeon themes indicate greater danger. Equipment degradation is the primary mechanical danger signal beyond enemy combat.
